results.title: Actions against Companies that Do Not Abide by Competition Commission's Directions
results.summary: Under the Competition Act 2004, the Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCS) can issue legally binding directions to prohibit, dissolve, or modify anti-competitive mergers. If a company fails to comply with a registered direction, it may be found in contempt of court. The Court may impose sanctions including a fine not exceeding $20,000 or imprisonment for up to 12 months, or both. CCS may also impose financial penalties on merger parties where warranted.

results.title: Additional Resource Allocation for Rising Domestic Violence Tier 1 Cases in Community Agencies
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development stated that no further updates are available regarding additional resource allocations for community agencies managing rising Tier 1 domestic violence cases or changes to average caseloads per social worker at Protection Specialist Centres. This response addresses a query raised on 5 November 2025 concerning the inaugural Domestic Violence Trends Report 2024, which indicated upward trends in Tier 1 cases. The joint answer from the Minister-in-charge of Social Services Integration confirms that the House has already received this information previously.

results.title: Address Verification under Primary School Registration Proximity Policy and Action Taken on Fraudulent Declarations
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) enforces the Primary 1 Registration Proximity Policy by requiring parents to declare their official NRIC address or eligible alternative addresses, such as yet-to-be-completed properties with a two-year move-in requirement. Fraudulent declarations are detected through regular school checks and public reports, with MOE rigorously investigating cases where evidence of wrongdoing exists. Upon discovery of fraud, the Ministry transfers the affected child to another school with available vacancies based on the actual residential address while assisting the family in settling in. Cases involving false information given to a public servant are referred to the Police for investigation under the Penal Code, as providing such information is a criminal offence punishable by law.

results.title: Additional Green and Blue Recycling Bins Given Rising Rate of Packaging Waste
results.summary: The National Environment Agency (NEA) ensures sufficient green and blue recycling bins in HDB estates by directing Public Waste Collectors (PWCs) to increase bin numbers and collection frequency to meet demand, a practice routinely applied during peak periods like festive seasons. For Town Councils, NEA collaborates on refuse collection where some have already requested additional bins or collections to address local needs, resulting in corresponding directives to PWCs. Additionally, Town Councils can procure extra green refuse bins for common areas as required. The Minister confirmed these mechanisms exist to align bin provision with locality-specific waste generation rates.

results.title: Addressing Loneliness and Psychological Distress among Seniors Living Alone
results.summary: One-person households among seniors aged 65 and above more than doubled from 42,100 in 2014 to 87,200 in 2024, with older adults living alone twice as likely to report depressive symptoms. Under Age Well SG, the Ministry of Health strengthens community-based detection by drawing seniors out of their homes for active ageing programmes. The Silver Generation Office conducts outreach to identify at-risk individuals and connects them to Active Ageing Centres for buddying and befriending services. Seniors with psychological needs are referred to community mental health teams for assessment and support, aligning these efforts with Singapore's broader preventive health strategy.

results.title: Addressing Low Attendance Rate Among ComLink+ Children in Anchor Operator Preschools and Intervention Measures
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development stated that the question regarding barriers to attendance and planned interventions for ComLink+ children in anchor operator preschools was previously addressed in the reply to Parliamentary Question No 60 for oral answer on the Order Paper for 12 January 2026. No new measures, statistics, or timelines were provided in this session. The specific details on primary barriers and intervention plans remain as outlined in the referenced parliamentary response.

results.title: Addressing Social Media Harms and Strengthening Student Cyber Resilience
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that research indicates excessive social media use and inappropriate content can negatively impact youth mental well-being. To address this, the Ministry integrates the recently released Government Positive Use Guide on Technology and Social Media into Cyber Wellness lessons across primary and secondary schools. These lessons equip students with skills to manage online activities, recognize digital risks, and discern negative influences. Beyond the classroom, schools collaborate with parents via the Parents Gateway platform to share resources and strategies for monitoring children's technology use. The Minister emphasized that parents play a key role in guiding their child's online activities alongside school initiatives.

results.title: Aligning Senior Management Incentive Structures at Temasek Holdings and GIC with Government Investment Mandates
results.summary: Senior management incentives at Temasek Holdings and GIC are determined by their respective boards as commercial entities, with no direct government interference in operational remuneration decisions. Both institutions maintain market-competitive frameworks designed to balance long-term value creation, prudent risk-taking, and capital preservation. The Government retains the role of assessing entity performance against investment mandates net of fees and expenses, rather than dictating specific incentive structures.

results.title: Amount of Opportunity Fund Grants Disbursed to Schools in Last Three Years
results.summary: Over the last three years, the Ministry of Education disbursed an average of $10 million annually in Opportunity Fund grants to General Education schools. Eligible students under the Ministry of Education Financial Assistance Scheme (MOE FAS) and School-based Financial Assistance Scheme (SBFAS) receive these grants automatically without application. Approximately 50,000 students benefit annually. For students not covered by these schemes, schools inform parents to seek assessment for financial assistance. The Minister did not disclose the specific percentage of eligible lower-income students who have utilized the fund or detailed measures ensuring awareness.

results.title: Animal Welfare Groups' Involvement in Trap-Neuter-Rehome/Release-Manage Programme
results.summary: Since 2018, the Animal and Veterinary Service (AVS) has partnered with Animal Welfare Groups (AWGs) to manage free-roaming dogs via the Trap-Neuter-Rehome/Release-Manage (TNRM) programme, with AVS intervening swiftly for public safety risks. AVS and its partners are refining joint protocols through regular stakeholder engagements, workshops, and feedback channels, including a scheduled workshop on 15 January. The programme has trapped 4,470 dogs, rehomed 2,048, and sterilised 80% of the population, exceeding the 70% target and reducing feedback cases by 80%. A review of the programme is underway to further improve coordination and collaboration between AVS, NParks, and AWGs.

results.title: Annual Tobacco Tax Revenue Collection Trends over Last Three Years
results.summary: Annual tobacco duty revenue collections for the past three financial years are published on the Singapore Customs website. The Minister for Finance provided tabulated data showing the specific revenue figures and the calculated rate of increase over this period. Users can access the full statistics directly via the official government portal to analyze the trends. No further commentary or projections were offered in the response.

results.title: Appeals Mechanism for Incoming Travellers with ICA's Upcoming No-Boarding Directives
results.summary: Travellers issued No-Boarding Directives (NBD) may appeal for entry via ICA's website feedback channel, though no immediate appeal mechanism exists. Approved appeals allow re-booking flights. NBDs commence at air checkpoints on 30 January 2026 and sea checkpoints by end-2028. Land checkpoints will not implement NBDs currently due to operational constraints preventing pre-boarding screening for road travellers arriving in vehicles or public transport.

results.title: Appeals Received for Silver Support Scheme and Publicity Effort to Raise Awareness for Scheme's Intentions
results.summary: From 2023 to 2025, the Ministry received an average of 11,300 annual appeals for the Silver Support Scheme, representing roughly 4% of recipients. Common appeals arise from changes in residence or per capita household income, prompting automatic eligibility reassessments when seniors update their details with the CPF Board. To enhance awareness, the Ministry disseminates information via Community Centres and Silver Generation Ambassadors, while all eligible seniors receive annual notification letters in English and vernacular languages. Seniors can verify eligibility status or access scheme details through the CPF Board's webpage, hotline, or service centres.

results.title: Assessment of GIC's Reference Portfolio Strategies and Framework to Evaluate Investment Performance
results.summary: The Minister for Finance directed that the question regarding the Government's assessment of GIC's Reference Portfolio appropriateness and alternative investment performance metrics be answered via the oral reply to Parliamentary Questions 5 to 11 filed in today's Order Paper. No new substantive details, specific metrics, or updated timelines were provided during this exchange. The response relies entirely on the referenced parliamentary document for the requested information.

results.title: Assessment of Traffic Noise Exposure in Residential Areas and Plans to Mitigate Long-Term Impacts of Environmental Noise on Residents
results.summary: The National Environment Agency (NEA) conducts Traffic Noise Impact Assessments for new residential developments near expressways and viaducts, projecting future noise levels to recommend mitigation measures that meet Technical Guidelines limits. These assessments cover both existing and new land traffic noise sources. NEA will periodically review the Technical Guidelines with other agencies to ensure continued relevance. The response did not specify a timeline for the next review or confirm if current criteria already account for long-term impacts on sleep and mental wellbeing.

results.title: Average Waiting Times for Appointments with Radiographers and Therapists at Public Hospitals
results.summary: The Ministry of Health does not track national-level waiting times for allied health services, noting that durations vary by patient condition severity. Public healthcare institutions report median waiting times ranging from within a day to up to two months for diagnostic radiographers, occupational therapists, physiotherapists, radiation therapists, and speech-language therapists. These intervals depend on individual clinical needs rather than a standardized national metric.

results.title: Awareness and Take-up Rate of Frontline Referrals and Community Development and Welfare Fund among Low-income Households
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth clarified that the Community Development and Welfare Fund (CDWF) is administered by Grassroots Organisations (GROs) to deliver social assistance, including grocery distributions, educational bursaries, and direct welfare support. Awareness and access are promoted through local estate publicity, social media, and partnerships with schools and Social Service Agencies for referrals. The People's Association (PA) leverages its extensive network of Community Clubs, digital channels, and Grassroots Leaders to identify residents in need via regular interactions and neighbour networks. The Minister confirmed that PA will continue to strengthen outreach efforts and collaborate with partners to connect eligible residents to appropriate assistance, though specific quantitative metrics on current awareness levels or take-up rates were not provided in the response.

results.title: Background Checks on Educators and Non-teaching Staff in Government Schools
results.summary: The Ministry of Education conducts pre-employment background checks on all prospective educators and non-teaching staff in Government schools to ensure student safety. These screenings include verifying past criminal records with the Police, conducting reference checks with previous employers, and performing credit assessments specifically for finance-related positions. The measures apply uniformly across primary, secondary, and post-secondary institutions as part of the standard hiring process.

results.title: Background Checks on Educators and Non-teaching Staff of Kindergartens, Preschools and Early Childhood Education Centres
results.summary: Preschool operators must obtain Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) approval for all staff, triggering ECDA checks to ensure individuals do not pose safety risks or have prior bars. Applicants must complete a self-declaration form detailing any prior offences or police warnings and submit a pre-employment medical report certifying fitness for work. This medical report requires a compulsory declaration of psychiatric history; if declared, a medical practitioner's certification of suitability to work with young children is mandatory. Employers also conduct independent recruitment procedures like interviews and reference checks.

results.title: Bilateral Economic Engagement Strategies with Newly Admitted ASEAN Member Timor Leste
results.summary: Timor-Leste acceded to ASEAN on 26 October 2025 and is implementing its Roadmap for Full Membership, a process requiring accession to all ASEAN treaties and domestic reforms to ensure an accessible economic environment. Singapore's 2024 trade with Timor-Leste totaled $89.9 million, with direct flights commencing that year and welcoming 3,668 visitors. The Government supports integration via the eSTARS package, offering customised programmes for ASEAN treaty accession, economic agreement assistance, leadership training, capacity building, and postgraduate scholarships aligned with Timor-Leste's development priorities. These efforts aim to foster trade and tourism cooperation between Singapore and Timor-Leste as the nascent market integrates into the ASEAN network.

results.title: Breakdown of Bird Species Tracked and Conservation Status of Bird Species in Forested Area East of Sungei Serangoon
results.summary: The National Parks Board tracks bird species conservation status via the Singapore Red Data Book and public sightings. According to the 2024 edition, 52 threatened bird species were reported in the area between Buangkok East Drive and Lorong Halus Wetland, located north of the planned bus depot site. The Ministry does not provide separate counts for total or migratory species in the specific Sungei Serangoon forested area requested.

results.title: Breakdown of Monthly Salary for Singaporeans Aged 60 and above in Full-time Employment as of December 2025
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that a breakdown of full-time employed residents by gross monthly income is published on the Ministry of Manpower's website. The specific data requested for Singaporeans aged 60 and over as of December 2025, categorized by salary brackets with and without employer CPF contributions, was not provided in the response. Users are directed to the official website to access the published statistics.

results.title: Breakdown of New Employment Pass Applications in Financial Services Sector and Non-financial Services Sectors in Last Five Years
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower increased Employment Pass minimum qualifying salaries from 2021 to 2025, raising the financial services sector threshold from $5,000 to $6,200 and the non-financial sector threshold from $4,500 to $5,600. Concurrently, the median fixed monthly salary for new EP applications rose from $13,300 to $15,000 in financial services and from $8,000 to $9,200 in other sectors. The Minister confirmed that the qualifying salary applies to the vast majority of EP holders, with targeted flexibilities granted only in specific areas supporting economic priorities. Detailed percentile data for the last five years is provided in accompanying tables.

results.title: Centralised SGVoucher Digital Wallet to Improve Accessibility
results.summary: The Minister for Digital Development and Information acknowledged the suggestion to create a centralised SGVoucher digital wallet to improve accessibility for future Government voucher disbursements. She stated that the Government will consider this proposal while refining its digital disbursement strategies. Currently, support is disbursed via various platforms, with RedeemSG serving as a general voucher system for schemes like CDC and SG60 vouchers. This system allows citizens to redeem digital and paper vouchers without downloading an app, though each tranche remains accessible through unique links. The Minister noted that the Government recognises varying citizen needs and comfort levels with technology when determining the most effective delivery methods.

results.title: Children Who Did Not Secure Placement in 2025 Primary 1 Registration Exercise
results.summary: All Singaporean children participating in the 2025 Primary 1 Registration Exercise secured a national primary school placement for 2026. Approximately 2,880 Singaporean children did not participate, primarily because they reside overseas, seek exemption from compulsory education, or are applying for or have received placement in special education schools. The Minister for Education confirmed these reasons account for the non-participating cohort. No other children were left without a placement.

results.title: Clarification by Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs clarified a statistic from a written answer regarding the SGSecure movement. The Ministry corrected the cited figure from the SGSecure Public Perception Survey of 2024, stating that 74% of respondents felt alert enough to identify suspicious behaviour or items, rather than the previously reported 77%. This correction addresses a Parliamentary Question filed by Mr Saktiandi Supaat on 26 September last year. No other policy measures or operational changes were announced during this clarification.

results.title: Codification of "Agony of the Moment" Defence in Penal Code 1871 for Negligent Driving Prosecutions
results.summary: The Minister for Home Affairs stated that codifying the "agony of the moment" defence in the Penal Code 1871 is unnecessary. The existing standard of care under the Road Traffic Act and Penal Code already requires drivers to act as a reasonable and prudent person would. This standard inherently assesses whether a driver reacted reasonably to unforeseeable circumstances beyond their control. Consequently, no offence of negligent driving is disclosed if the driver acted reasonably, rendering a specific statutory defence redundant.

results.title: Comparison of Unit Costs and Energy Intensity of Singapore's Water Sources over Past Five Years and Balancing Water Security with Affordability
results.summary: Desalinated water remains more energy-intensive and costly than NEWater or imported water but serves as a critical weather-resilient National Tap. The sixth desalination plant will utilize advanced technologies like ultra-permeable and ceramic membranes to lower production costs and energy intensity. While PUB requires infrastructure investments to meet growing demand, water conservation efforts aim to moderate demand growth and reduce the need for additional costly infrastructure. The Government balances water security with affordability by leveraging these efficiency gains and conservation measures.

results.title: Considerations for Declassification Policy to Include Mechanism for Researchers to Nominate Documents for Review
results.summary: Following the declassification of the Albatross Files, the Ministry for Digital Development and Information confirmed that a formal mechanism exists for researchers to nominate documents for review under the National Library Board Act. Any person may request access to public archives transferred to the National Archives of Singapore (NAS) if they are over 25 years old and deemed of national or historical significance. The relevant Government agency decides on access, weighing research support against national security, confidentiality, and privacy concerns. The NAS facilitates this review process, allowing historians to formally nominate records. The Ministry will continue collaborating with NAS and agencies to assess nominated records and progressively release more public records to strengthen national identity.

results.title: Construction of Wildlife Exclusion Fences between Central Catchment Nature Reserve and Lorong Asrama Forest in View of Enhancing Ecological Connectivity
results.summary: To mitigate vehicular-wildlife collisions, the National Parks Board announced earlier this year it would extend exclusion fences to nine additional locations, including major roads along the Central Catchment Nature Reserve. These high-risk sites were identified based on past incidents. The fences, developed with wildlife groups and academic experts, guide wildlife toward alternative crossings like the Eco-Link @ BKE and existing culverts, enhancing safety for movement between green spaces. The Minister did not address the specific request regarding rope bridges or culverts for the Lorong Asrama site.

results.title: Conversion of Excess Multi-storey Carpark Levels into Community Sports Facilities in Jalan Kayu-Fernvale
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board is collaborating with Sport Singapore to evaluate converting under-utilised upper decks of multi-storey car parks into community sports facilities for Jalan Kayu-Fernvale. This conversion is subject to detailed assessment regarding future parking demand, potential resident disamenities, and safety considerations. The Ministry will keep the specific request in mind as Sport Singapore progressively develops Sport-in-Precinct facilities across various communities. No immediate timeline or eligibility criteria for this specific conversion were stated in the response.

results.title: Crediting of HDB Flat Sale Proceeds into Relevant CPF Account for Older Divorcees
results.summary: When an HDB flat is sold, net proceeds first top up the Retirement Account (RA) to meet the Full Retirement Sum for members aged 55 and above; any remaining balance is credited to the Ordinary Account (OA) for subsequent housing purchases. Divorcees may use RA funds exceeding the Basic Retirement Sum to buy a 3-room or smaller flat if qualifying conditions are met. The CPF Board reviews appeals on a case-by-case basis to allow greater RA usage or larger flat purchases based on financial circumstances, balancing housing needs with retirement adequacy. Divorcing couples can explore options via the Housing Options Explorer on the Family Assist Portal or seek advice from the HDB.

results.title: Data on Court Applications Filed against Suppliers for Unfair Practices over Past Two Years
results.summary: From 2024 to 2025, the Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCS) resolved 11 unfair practice cases under the Consumer Protection (Fair Trading) Act 2003 without court intervention. During this period, CCS filed one application for court orders against two immigration consultancy businesses for misleading practices. No further court applications were filed against suppliers for unfair practices in the specified timeframe. Details on the immigration consultancy case are available on the CCS website.

results.title: Data on Elderly Recipients Aged 65 and above Receiving ComCare Long-Term Assistance and Short-to-Medium-Term Assistance in Last Three Years
results.summary: Based on the ComCare Trends Report FY2018 to FY2022, elderly beneficiaries (aged 65+) comprised 81% of Long-Term Assistance recipients and 9% to 10% of Short-to-Medium-Term Assistance recipients from FY2020 to FY2022. The Ministry of Social and Family Development ceased calculating these proportions from FY2023 due to stable figures and limited analytical value. Concurrent receipt data for Silver Support Scheme recipients is unavailable.

results.title: Data on Foreigners with Valid In-Principle Approvals Who Were Denied Entry
results.summary: The Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA) does not track the number of foreigners with valid In-Principle Approvals (IPAs) denied entry, as an IPA does not guarantee entry and all arrivals undergo security and immigration risk assessment. With the No-Boarding Directive implementation later this month, prospective employers may be informed if an IPA holder is prevented from boarding flights, a process similar to current denial upon arrival. Affected individuals must inform their employers and appeal via the ICA feedback channel before arranging a new flight to work in Singapore. The outcome remains unchanged from current operations where entry is neither automatic nor a right.

results.title: Data on Platform Workers Opting In for CPF Contributions and Top Reasons for Not Opting In
results.summary: As of September 2025, approximately 26,500 platform workers born before 1 January 1995 have opted into CPF contributions following the Platform Workers Act's implementation on 1 January 2025, representing 27% of the eligible cohort. The opt-in rate is higher at 32% among those relying on platform work as their primary or sole income source. The Ministry of Manpower does not collect data on reasons for not opting in and will continue collaborating with platform operators and associations to encourage participation.

results.title: Data on Subletting of HDB Shops Purchased by Foreign Buyers and Proposal to Impose ABSD on Foreign Buyers
results.summary: Less than 1% of the 8,500 privately-owned HDB shops are owned by foreign individuals, with no mandatory reporting requirement for subletting. The Minister clarified that Additional Buyer's Stamp Duty (ABSD) is designed to moderate residential property demand and stated there are currently no plans to impose ABSD on commercial properties, including non-residential HDB shop units. Consequently, the Ministry will not introduce ABSD on foreign buyers of HDB shops, regardless of whether the units contain a residential component.

results.title: Data on Unsafe Workplace Practices Complaints Made via SnapSAFE and Enforcement Actions Taken against Companies
results.summary: In 2025, the Ministry of Manpower received approximately 1,700 unsafe workplace practice complaints via SnapSAFE. Of all reports filed in 2024 and 2025, about 70% resulted in enforcement actions, including warnings issued to companies. The remaining reports were either insufficient for follow-up or did not identify work-related lapses. This data reflects increased reporting willingness following the 2023 "Reporting Saves Lives" campaign.

results.title: Data on Unutilised SkillsFuture Credits and Plans for Redeployment of Unutilised Funds
results.summary: Data on unutilised SkillsFuture Credits as of end-2025 will be released in February 2026, covering the number of eligible Singaporeans who have not, partially, or fully utilised the credit, along with the total unutilised amount. The Minister for Education confirmed that specific figures for these metrics are pending publication. Details regarding the Ministry's plans to redeploy any unutilised funds were not provided in the response.

results.title: Detection of Tunnel Squatting along CTE, MCE and KPE Road Tunnels and Enhancement of Road and Subway Tunnel Safety Standards
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) reports no significant deformation in Singapore's rectangular road tunnels, which are less susceptible to squatting than circular bored tunnels in softer ground. Current safety standards align with international best practices comparable to Hong Kong, Shanghai, and Tokyo. Comprehensive protocols require developers to obtain LTA approval, deploy monitoring instruments, and conduct regular inspections for nearby construction works. LTA audits ensure these measures are properly implemented to protect rail and road infrastructure.

results.title: Determining Preschools' Operational Status after Reports of Child Mismanagement or Abuse
results.summary: The Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) determines preschool operational status based on a proportionate penalty framework for regulatory breaches like child mismanagement. Penalties may include financial fines, licence shortening, or revocation, assessed by breach severity, systemic nature, and the preschool's ability to ensure child safety. Licence revocation is a last resort due to impacts on families and livelihoods, with child safety remaining a fundamental priority. ECDA continues to collaborate with operators to enforce compliance and safeguard children's well-being.

results.title: Developments towards Singapore's Digital Warehouse Receipt Systems and Metal Warrant Financing Framework
results.summary: Most critical minerals are stored and financed via the London Metal Exchange (LME) ecosystem, where LME member banks accept LME digital receipts as collateral. Some companies use non-LME Singapore warehouses for lower costs or end-use, but these typically issue physical receipts due to a lack of commercial incentive for separate local digital platforms. EnterpriseSG will review the need to accredit trusted issuers under section 16Q of the Electronic Transactions Act only if sufficient market interest emerges. Metal warrant financing in Dubai and the UK similarly relies on internationally recognised exchange-based systems rather than standalone local frameworks.

results.title: Effectiveness of New Measures for Social Service Workforce in Improving Service and Support to Residents
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development announced three key measures to strengthen the social service workforce: enhanced Social Service Tribe Study Awards for associate-level qualifications, increased Professional Development Sponsorship funding from 75% to full funding, and expanded Foundational Leadership grants for first-time and senior leaders. These initiatives target social workers, counsellors, and psychologists to improve capacity in mental health and family support services. The Ministry tracks manpower needs, salaries, inflow, retention, and shortfalls while monitoring scheme uptake with agencies to ensure adequate resourcing and accessibility. No specific timeline for assessing the impact of these measures was stated in the response.

results.title: Employers Sanctioned for Submitting Fictitious Recruitment Records for Employment Pass Applications
results.summary: Between 2023 and 2025, the Ministry of Manpower sanctioned an average of 140 employers annually for submitting fictitious recruitment records or failing to fairly consider local candidates. Enforcement actions range from written warnings to debarment from hiring foreign workers for up to 24 months. The Ministry confirmed it will take action against foreign hiring managers found complicit in fraudulent activities, which may include revocation of work passes and debarment from employment in Singapore. These measures apply to employers using token measures like false MyCareersFuture advertisements to circumvent the work pass framework.

results.title: Enforcement of Employer Obligations for Provision of Accommodation and Food for Workers with Pending Employment Claims Tribunal Cases or Work Injury Compensation Claims
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that the enforcement of employers' obligations to provide acceptable accommodation and adequate food to Work Permit holders during pending Employment Claims Tribunal or Work Injury Compensation cases was addressed via a written reply to Question No 28 on the Order Paper for 6 November 2025. No new operational measures, timelines, or specific eligibility constraints were introduced in this session. The parliamentary record indicates the matter was handled through existing written response mechanisms rather than immediate legislative or policy changes.

results.title: Enhancing Public Transport Network Connectivity for Bukit Batok West Residents Travelling to Jurong East, Choa Chu Kang and Bukit Panjang Estates
results.summary: Under the Bus Connectivity Enhancement Programme, bus service 991 frequency was improved to shorten wait times for Bukit Batok West residents travelling to Choa Chu Kang. Service 984 was introduced in October 2025 to provide a direct bus route to Jurong East, complementing existing services 180 and 993 that connect to Bukit Panjang and Jurong East respectively. Future connectivity will be further enhanced by the Jurong Region Line, offering residents additional travel options to Jurong East, Choa Chu Kang, and Bukit Panjang estates.

results.title: Ensuring Short-form Reel-style Mini Movies and Series on Social Media Platforms are Suitable for General Audience
results.summary: Singapore's film classification framework does not apply to short-form reel-style videos on social media platforms. Instead, the Infocomm Media Development Authority directs designated services—including Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, X, and HardwareZone—to disable access to egregious content such as videos advocating sexual violence, suicide, or terrorism. Under the Code of Practice for Online Safety, these platforms must implement systems to mitigate exposure to harmful content and ensure children are not targeted with detrimental material. The Code also mandates more restrictive account settings for minors. Complementing these regulatory measures, the Ministry of Digital Development and Information has introduced the Positive Use Guide on Technology and Social Media to assist Singaporeans in navigating digital risks and promoting healthy technology usage.

results.title: Establishment of Psychological Aid and Counselling Support Unit for HDB Fire-Affected Residents
results.summary: The Ministry will not establish a dedicated Fire Victim Support Unit, as immediate psychological first aid is provided via medical assistance and long-term support is directed to the healthcare system. For fire-affected HDB residents, the Singapore Civil Defence Force conducts "Post Fire Blitz" sessions to engage the community on fire safety and promote resilience. People's Association grassroots organisations coordinate essential items like food and daily necessities, while the Housing and Development Board provides temporary accommodation where necessary. The Minister stated that existing mechanisms are sufficient to address trauma without creating a new unit.

results.title: Evaluation of Existing Fire Safety Measures on Repair and Renovations for High-Rise Buildings' External Walls
results.summary: The Singapore Civil Defence Force conducts regular enforcement checks to ensure building owners comply with fire safety requirements, mandating the use of only SCDF-approved materials for external features. The Ministry of Manpower also inspects worksites for compliance with Workplace Safety and Health Regulations regarding work-related fire risks. The Government is currently reviewing early observations from the Hong Kong Wang Fuk Court fire investigation to determine if strengthening regulations for construction and maintenance of residential buildings is necessary. The outcome of this ongoing review will be announced in due course.

results.title: Evaluation of Major Train Delay on North-South Line and Frequency of Similar Train Faults in Past Three Years
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated the December 26, 2025, North-South Line delay stemmed from a backup power system fault, marking the first occurrence of this specific issue. The Land Transport Authority and SMRT are collaborating with the train manufacturer to determine the root cause and implement preventive measures. Concurrently, incident response protocols regarding service recovery, commuter management, and public communications are under review for improvement. No similar faults have been recorded on the line in the preceding three years.

results.title: Existing Limits and Proposed Standards for Regulation of Emergency Vehicle Siren Noise Levels
results.summary: Emergency vehicle sirens in Singapore currently comply with the SAE J1849 international standard, which defines the minimum sound level required for effective warning. The Ministry states these devices are essential for prompt response to life-threatening situations and are activated only when necessary. No existing upper noise limits or specific residential impact constraints were detailed in the response. The Minister indicated that the current standard ensures audibility without specifying a review timeline for noise impact minimisation.

results.title: Expanding CDMP Coverage to Include High-Burden Autoimmune or Multisystem Conditions
results.summary: The Chronic Disease Management Programme (CDMP) excludes autoimmune conditions like lupus, Crohn's disease, and small fibre neuropathy because they are less prevalent and primarily managed by hospital specialists using complex therapies unsuitable for primary care. The Clinical Advisory Committee evaluates inclusion based on disease burden, primary care suitability, evidence-based guidelines, and cumulative patient costs. Although not on the CDMP, eligible patients currently receive up to 75% subsidies at Specialist Outpatient Clinics. Additionally, patients aged 60 and above can utilize Flexi-MediSave for treatments up to the prevailing withdrawal limit. The government has not yet confirmed future inclusion of high-burden conditions despite their clear benefits from early intervention.

results.title: Expanding Eligibility Criteria for SEP Bonus under COMPASS for Employment Pass Applications
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that the Complementarity Assessment Framework (COMPASS) awards Strategic Economic Priorities (SEP) bonus points to companies participating in specific government-supported programmes. Eligible initiatives include EnterpriseSG's Singapore Global Executive Programme for overseas work stints, the Singapore Tourism Board's Leadership Development Grant for local executive training, and collaborations with the National Trades Unions Congress to form Company Training Committees. Companies must engage in at least one of these activities to qualify for the bonus points, which support investments in local professionals, managers, and executives. No new expansion of eligibility criteria was proposed during the session.

results.title: Fire Safety Safeguards and Statutory Intervention Measures in Severe Public Housing Hoarding Cases
results.summary: HDB units function as fire compartments with ventilated escape routes, though severe hoarding obstructs these paths, creating evacuation risks. While agencies coordinate decluttering via the Hoarding Management Core Group and NEAT, compulsory clearance generally requires occupant cooperation unless public safety thresholds are crossed. Specifically, if hoarding impedes escape routes, the Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) may direct occupants to clear paths, with non-compliance punishable by fines up to $10,000 or imprisonment. As a last resort, the Director-General of the Community Relations Unit can apply to the Community Disputes Resolution Tribunal (CDRT) to order decluttering if prior orders are ignored and public interest warrants it. The Government will monitor fire situations and introduce further legislative measures if necessary.

results.title: Follow-up from Feedback on Disamenities from Bus Depot Construction Site at Forested Area between Tampines Road and Buangkok East Drive
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority confirmed the Tampines Road bus depot cannot be relocated but is implementing specific mitigation measures. A 12-metre-high temporary noise barrier will be erected to insulate residents, despite the site being over 200 metres away. Additionally, noise monitoring systems have been installed to record construction levels, while dust control includes spraying exposed surfaces with water and cleaning vehicles before they leave the site. These actions address resident feedback regarding disamenities from the ongoing construction activities.

results.title: Frequency of HPB and MOE's Canteen Vendor Compliance Audits for Healthy Meals in School Programme
results.summary: The Health Promotion Board (HPB) regularly assesses canteen vendor compliance with the Healthy Meals in Schools Programme (HMSP) guidelines from 2020 to 2024, where over half of stalls fully meet requirements. Common non-compliance areas involve the use of non-Healthier Choice Symbol sauces, meat-based products, or noodles. HPB shares assessment results with schools and vendors, providing recommendations and resources like the HMSP Toolkit for Schools to address improvements. Specific audit frequency, coverage percentages, and enforcement actions were not detailed in the response.

results.title: Furthering Singapore's Foreign Policy Objectives through Foreign Disaster Assistance and Rescue Missions and Plans to Expand Contingent Sizes
results.summary: Singapore's Civil Defence Force (SCDF) supports foreign policy objectives through Operation Lionheart, a 24/7 ready contingent of 80 personnel and four search dogs providing urban search and rescue. This team includes regular officers, full-time National Servicemen, and operationally ready National Servicemen, recently deployed to Myanmar in March 2025 following an earthquake to conduct search and rescue, recover victims, and establish medical outposts. For major regional disasters, SCDF maintains protocols to activate additional standby personnel and deploy extra OLH teams as required. The Minister clarified that these contributions demonstrate solidarity, support affected populations, and promote bilateral ties while maintaining operational flexibility for rapid mobilisation during significant humanitarian crises.

results.title: Healthcare Support for Young Adults with Chronic Conditions outside CDMP Coverage
results.summary: Outpatient subsidies at public Specialist Outpatient Clinics are differentiated by per capita household income rather than age, with lower-income patients accessing additional support like MediFund for complex conditions. MediSave can be used for costly outpatient treatments, including diagnostic scans for conditions outside the Chronic Disease Management Programme list, and is extended to cover clinically and cost-effective screenings. Starting 1 January 2026, the annual MediSave limit for scans will increase from $300 to $600. The Minister noted that these measures provide flexibility for younger adults with chronic conditions requiring specialist care.

results.title: Help for Farms Given Switch from "30 by 30" Goal to New Targets for Fibre and Protein Productions by 2035
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment will address the transition of farms from the original "30 by 30" goals to new 2035 fibre and protein targets, including any excluded food types and the rationale behind them, in the next Sitting on 13 January 2026. This response will also cover available enhanced grants or offtake schemes to assist farms whose produce is less aligned with the revised targets. The upcoming reply aims to provide a holistic clarification on whether the new targets are aspirational or realistically achievable alongside details on support mechanisms.

results.title: Fire Safety in Our Homes
results.summary: Singapore's fire safety landscape remains stable with low fatalities over the past five years, as most incidents stem from human behaviour like unattended cooking rather than infrastructure gaps. While rising fatalities in 2023–2025 and concerns over older estates prompted questions on applying SCDF Fire Code Clause 2.3.9(a) to residential units, officials affirmed the existing code's robustness and the effectiveness of fire compartmentalisation. Retrofitting older flats is deemed limited by practicality and cost, though single-exit staircases in point blocks are considered viable due to low occupancy. The government also addressed AMD risks, noting that battery swapping systems and public charging hubs require further evaluation, while dry riser inspection frequencies are currently adequate.

results.title: Holding Preschool Operators Accountable for Deployment of Staff Not Qualified for Child-minding Duties and Adequacy of Current Measures
results.summary: Preschool operators must comply with the Early Childhood Development Centre Act, securing Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) approval for all deployed staff and establishing safety systems. ECDA enforces accountability through financial penalties, corrective action plans, or licence revocation for operators, while imposing administrative actions like retraining or approval cancellation for unqualified staff. The Ministry for Social and Family Development and ECDA prioritize child safety, having strengthened safeguards over recent years. They will continue reviewing and enhancing enforcement measures as necessary, though no specific timeline for publishing review findings was provided in the response.

results.title: Impact of "Ghost Jobs" on Job Mobility
results.summary: Lower job mobility in 2025 stems primarily from reduced labour turnover and resignation rates following moderated labour market tightness, which limits replacement vacancies and recruitment activity. While job postings may close without hires due to legitimate business changes or inability to find suitable candidates, a small number of instances involve token vacancies to meet fair consideration rules for locals. The Ministry of Manpower will investigate such allegations upon receiving feedback and take enforcement action against errant employers if findings are confirmed to safeguard fair hiring practices.

results.title: Impact on SME Tenants following Major Acquisitions of Heartland Malls
results.summary: The Minister clarified that commercial tenancy mix and turnover result from market decisions by owners and tenants, noting that the CapitaLand–Mapletree merger parties have not formally notified the Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCS) for assessment, though CCS will continue monitoring. To support fair lease negotiations, the Government enforces the Code of Conduct for Leasing of Retail Premises under the Lease Agreements for Retail Premises Act since February 2024, which prohibits unreasonable clauses like electricity cost markups. Disputes may be resolved via the Singapore Mediation Centre. Enterprise Singapore supports SMEs through grants such as the Productivity Solutions Grant and the Enterprise Development Grant, alongside programmes like the Retail Maverick Challenge and Design Orchard Retail Singapore to foster innovation and differentiation.

results.title: Implementation of Long-term Maintenance and Renewal Strategies to Enhance MRT Resilience over Past Five Years
results.summary: Over the past five years, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) has implemented long-term maintenance and renewal strategies for the MRT network, replacing rolling stock and signalling systems based on original equipment manufacturers' recommended service lives, historical performance, and ongoing support availability. The LTA and rail operators conduct periodic technology upgrades and install condition monitoring tools to enable predictive maintenance and early fault detection. These asset management practices align with the international ISO 55000 standard, which mandates regular performance evaluation and institutionalized risk assessment frameworks. Metro operators in cities like Hong Kong and London also benchmark their practices against this standard. The Rail Reliability Taskforce has submitted recommendations to prioritize core system renewals and enhance condition monitoring usage. The Ministry of Transport is currently reviewing these recommendations and will provide a response by the first quarter of this year.

results.title: Implications of 15-month Cooling Period for High-Value HDB Flat Sellers versus Private Property Sellers
results.summary: The 15-month wait-out period for private property owners buying HDB resale flats was introduced in September 2022 as a temporary measure to moderate demand and prioritize affordable housing for urgent needs like first-time buyers. This policy applies regardless of realized capital gains, distinguishing it from high-value HDB flat sellers who may immediately repurchase. Seniors purchasing 4-room or smaller flats are exempted, and HDB accedes to about one in four appeals from households facing extenuating circumstances on a case-by-case basis. The government intends to remove the requirement when market conditions allow, continuing to monitor the resale market for adjustments.

results.title: Inclusion of Policies on Managing Heat Stress and Driving Fatigue in WSH Guidelines for Migrant Workers and Commercial Drivers for High-risk Outdoor and Transport Sectors
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower and WSH Council incorporated lessons from the Singapore Armed Forces and DSO National Laboratories into updated Workplace Safety and Health guidelines for high-risk sectors. For heat stress, the new measures include acclimatisation programmes, rest breaks, and recognition of heat illness symptoms. Regarding driving fatigue, the guidelines establish a fatigue reporting mechanism, mandate sufficient rest periods in work schedules, and require identification of fatigue symptoms. These recommendations apply specifically to commercial drivers and migrant workers in outdoor and transport roles. The guidelines were developed in consultation with key stakeholders, including relevant Government agencies.

results.title: Incorporating Declassified Albatross File Materials into Educational Curricula, Its Timeline and Teachers' Training
results.summary: The Ministry of Education will incorporate declassified Albatross File materials into Social Studies, History, and National Education curricula in an age-appropriate manner to enrich student inquiry and foster nuanced understanding of Singapore's separation from Malaysia. Updates to textbooks and digital resources occur periodically as new evidence-based interpretations emerge, with prior releases since the late 1990s already reflecting the negotiated separation. Teachers will receive guidance through updated subject-specific teaching guides and dedicated workshops focused on handling historical sources, evidence, and differing interpretations. This approach ensures students learn that valid historical conclusions require substantiation by sound reasoning and may evolve with newly discovered sources.

results.title: Key Non-financial Barriers to Regular Preschool Attendance
results.summary: The Minister identified non-financial barriers to preschool attendance for lower-income families, including irregular employment hours, caregiving demands, and medical issues. To address these, the Ministry deploys family coaches to ComLink+ families who monitor attendance, assist in securing flexible employment, and establish structured routines. Additional support includes payouts to the Child Development Account under the ComLink+ Progress Package (Preschool) and collaboration with grassroots organisations like MENDAKI to reinforce attendance norms. The Early Childhood Development Agency also partners with selected anchor operator preschool centres to provide targeted attendance and learning support for children from lower-income families, aiming to ensure every child has a good start in life.

results.title: Lasting Powers of Attorney Amendments and Revocations by Family Justice Courts and Donee Age Group in Last Five Years
results.summary: Under the Mental Capacity Act, Lasting Powers of Attorney (LPA) cannot be amended after execution; a donor must revoke and replace the document to effect changes. Consequently, donees cannot revoke an LPA. Between 2021 and 2025, the Family Justice Courts recorded 4,379 LPAs revoked and replaced by donors, with 3,799 donors aged 50 or above. Additionally, four LPAs were revoked via court order during this period. No data was provided for revocations by donees as this action is legally prohibited.

results.title: Lessons from Hong Kong's High-rise Fire for Singapore's Review of Building (Strata Management) Act
results.summary: The Government is reviewing the Building (Strata Management) Act to incorporate early observations from the ongoing Hong Kong Wang Fuk Court fire investigation. While the full inquiry remains active, relevant findings will be integrated into Singapore's legislative and policy framework. The Ministry of National Development will specifically examine Management Corporation Strata Title powers regarding large-scale infrastructure repairs. Further details on strengthening oversight for management councils will be shared in due course.

results.title: Linkage of Singpass Accounts to Valid Mobile Phone Numbers
results.summary: Singpass requires users to verify mobile numbers via one-time passwords (OTPs) during registration and account updates to confirm access, while implementing facial verification for high-risk transactions or detected anomalies to mitigate unauthorised access risks. The Government will continue collaborating with relevant agencies and industry partners to assess the need for additional measures to strengthen mobile number validation. Users remain responsible for updating their personal data with transacting organisations. No specific platform for telecommunication providers to update deregistered numbers was confirmed; the Minister indicated ongoing assessment rather than an immediate implementation of such a platform.

results.title: Maintenance, Performance Optimisation and Control Deactivations of Electric Public Bus Fleets in Absence of "Over-the-air" Updates
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) employs a risk-tiered cybersecurity framework for electric public buses, mandating compliance with UN Regulations 155 and 156 which require certified controls for secure, authenticated, and traceable updates. Under the Road Traffic Act, manufacturers must declare Over-The-Air (OTA) capabilities during approval, while significant operational modifications require prior LTA consent. Currently, all software updates for the public bus fleet are executed on-site via wired connections by authorised personnel after LTA verification, as OEMs have assured the absence of remote command capabilities pending independent technical assessments. LTA is collaborating with government cybersecurity agencies to develop a safe transition from wired to OTA updates to address emerging vulnerabilities.

results.title: Managing Healthcare Insurance Premiums
results.summary: The Ministry of Health will implement new Integrated Shield Plan (IP) rider requirements effective 27 November 2025, targeting policies purchased on or after this date to restore insurance sustainability. New riders will feature approximately 30% lower premiums but exclude minimum deductibles and impose higher co-payments, capped at 5% up to $6,000 annually, with MediSave expected to cover most costs for six in ten claimants. While insurers retain discretion for pre-November 2025 policies, the government assures that public subsidised healthcare remains universally accessible if private coverage becomes unaffordable. MOH and MAS will jointly oversee insurer conduct to prevent market failure, though premium adjustments remain commercial decisions. The policy aims to curb over-consumption driven by excessive coverage, which has contributed to rising private healthcare costs and a decade-long shift of patients from private to public hospitals.

results.title: Mandating Benchmarks for Restructured Temasek Entities and Regular Disclosure of Investment Returns and Costs
results.summary: The Minister for Finance directed that the specific mandates, return targets, and benchmarks for newly restructured Temasek entities are detailed in the oral replies to Parliamentary Questions No 12 to 14 filed in today's Order Paper. Regarding the request for regular publication of relative investment returns and costs, the Ministry has not provided a separate commitment beyond referencing the existing oral replies. Consequently, no new disclosure framework or timeline for these metrics was established during this session.

results.title: Mandatory Psychological Assessments and Background Checks for All Preschool Staff Including Non-teaching Personnel
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development clarified that the Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) does not impose mandatory psychological assessments for preschool staff, including non-teaching personnel. Instead, all staff must undergo a pre-employment medical check certified by a practitioner to confirm fitness to work with children, which includes a compulsory declaration of any history of psychiatric conditions. ECDA conducts background checks on all applicants to ensure they do not pose a risk to children's safety or have been barred from employment. Individuals providing false information regarding offences or psychiatric history face penalties under the Early Childhood Development Centres Act, including fines up to $5,000, imprisonment up to six months, or cancellation of deployment approval.

results.title: Lower Investment Returns Reported by Temasek Holdings and GIC
results.summary: The Government clarified that global rankings are unhelpful for assessing Temasek Holdings and GIC because they ignore unique mandates and contexts. While concerns were raised regarding lower risk-adjusted returns relative to benchmarks and the sustainability of Net Investment Returns Contribution (NIRC) amidst market volatility, officials emphasized that investment mandates have evolved to balance capital preservation with real returns. The response noted that active management strategies remain necessary to generate alpha, though the framework for reviewing NIRC thresholds and capital allocation in a fragmenting geopolitical environment is under review. Additionally, inquiries about investing more in local infrastructure and evaluating local strategic portfolios separately were addressed by highlighting the distinct objectives of these entities compared to international holdings.

results.title: Market Share and Pricing Safeguards for Private Equity-backed Nursing Homes
results.summary: The merger of Singapore's two largest private nursing home operators will result in a combined market share of approximately 10%. The Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore cleared the deal, finding no substantial loss of competition. Under the Build-Own-Lease model, the Government funds capital costs and appoints operators via competitive tenders, which lowers entry barriers and supports diversity. Government operating subventions are distributed proportionately to private operators based on their subsidised market share. No specific safeguards against future price increases were detailed in the response.

results.title: Measures to Support Local Businesses against Competition from Overseas Chains
results.summary: As of 8 January 2026, the Ministry provided a breakdown of registered retail entities by ownership. Enterprise Singapore supports local retailers through the Productivity Solutions Grant, Enterprise Development Grant, and Market Readiness Assistance Grant to enhance productivity and expand overseas. Targeted initiatives include the Retail Maverick Challenge, a platform for piloting innovative concepts, and Design Orchard Retail Singapore (DORS), which showcases over 80 local design brands with shared facilities to reduce costs. The Government will continue collaborating with stakeholders like the Singapore Retailers Association to explore further support measures for local businesses competing against overseas chains.

results.title: Ministry's Support for Family Resilience and Initiatives to Navigate Challenges while Promoting Work-Life Balance
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) reports that 85.9% of families demonstrated moderate to high resilience in 2023, supported by the Families For Life (FFL) movement which rolled out community programmes across all 24 towns by 2025. Key initiatives include marriage preparation, the Journey with You mentoring scheme, and parenting programmes designed to foster strong relationships. To promote work-life harmony, MSF encourages Flexible Work Arrangements and family-friendly workplaces, guided by Tripartite Guidelines. Parental leave provisions are being enhanced, with a new Shared Parental Leave scheme fully implemented from 1 April 2026, offering eligible couples 30 weeks of paid leave in the child's first year. Additional support is available via the Family Assist portal and the Strengthening Families Programme for couples facing relationship challenges.

results.title: Monitoring of and Investigations into Illegal Short-term Rental Listings on Online Platforms
results.summary: The Urban Redevelopment Authority (URA) actively monitors online platforms for illegal short-term rentals in private residential properties and enforces actions ranging from warnings to court prosecution. Since 2017, URA has investigated approximately 3,600 suspected cases, resulting in 35 prosecutions for egregious violations. The Ministry does not disclose the total number of public reports received, focusing instead on the volume of investigated cases and successful prosecutions.

results.title: More Measures to Reduce Teachers' Non-teaching Workload
results.summary: The Minister for Education directed inquiries regarding additional measures to reduce teachers' non-teaching workload to a previous response delivered on 4 November 2025. That prior response, covering the Teaching and Learning International Survey (TALIS) 2024 findings and management of teachers' workload and well-being, is available on the Ministry of Education's website. No new specific steps, timelines, or eligibility rules were announced in this current sitting.

results.title: Piloting Installation of Fire-rated Outdoor Charging Cabinets within Communal Facilities for PMDs
results.summary: The Minister for National Development declined to pilot fire-rated outdoor charging cabinets in HDB void decks or multi-storey carparks, noting that overseas models may not suit Singapore's context due to space trade-offs. He emphasized that preventing fires is more effective than relocating charging locations, as non-compliant devices pose risks regardless of where they are charged. Consequently, the primary safety strategy involves ensuring Active Mobility Devices meet prescribed technical requirements, with the Ministry of Transport and Land Transport Authority enforcing strict regulations on non-compliant imports and sales. The government plans to strengthen regulations on online AMD sales later this year and urges users to purchase compliant devices, avoid modifications, and adopt safe-charging practices to mitigate fire risks.

results.title: Plans to Increase Licensed Allied Health Professionals Beyond Current Growth Trend
results.summary: The number of Allied Health Professionals (AHPs) regulated by the Allied Health Professions Council (AHPC) has grown from 6,000 in 2019 to 8,000 in 2024, with projections indicating a continued ~6% annual increase to meet ageing population needs. Current registered categories include diagnostic radiographers, occupational therapists, physiotherapists, radiation therapists, and speech-language therapists. The Ministry periodically evaluates risk profiles to determine if expanding regulated professions is necessary, with a specific focus on requiring registration for psychologists providing direct care involving higher-risk assessments and interventions.

results.title: Policy Review of Cardiovascular Health Prevention, Treatments and Expansion of Community-based Rehabilitation Programmes
results.summary: The Ministry of Health provides comprehensive cardiovascular care through Healthier SG, which offers fully subsidised screening for eligible Singapore Citizens and extended subsidies for genetic testing for Familial Hypercholesterolaemia. Structured follow-up care for post-heart attack patients includes regular reviews at Specialist Outpatient Clinics and primary care settings, with referrals to cardiac rehabilitation programmes at Public Healthcare Institutions or social service agencies. Financial support includes up to 75% subsidies for drugs on the Standard Drug List and Medication Assistance Fund list, annual MediSave withdrawals of up to $700 for chronic disease management, and an additional $400 per year for seniors aged 60 and above via the Flexi-MediSave scheme. Patients facing affordability challenges after subsidies may apply for MediFund assistance.

results.title: Population Planning Criteria to Determine Construction of New Mosques
results.summary: MUIS determines new mosque construction using a prudent, sustainable approach that considers projected population changes, land development plans, and actual worship patterns. These patterns explicitly include Muslim residents, local citizens, and foreign workers to ensure adequate prayer space supply. When demand exists, MUIS collaborates with mosques and agencies to provide additional spaces through dual Friday prayer sessions or dormitory prayer areas. Funding for new mosques comes from the community via the Mosque Building and MENDAKI Fund (MBMF). The Minister confirmed MUIS continues to support the Muslim community in fulfilling religious obligations.

results.title: Possible Revisions to History and Social Studies Syllabuses in light of Declassification of Albatross Files
results.summary: The Minister for Education directed members to refer to existing written and oral replies for details on syllabus amendments regarding the declassified Albatross Files. Specifically, part (a) of the query was addressed in the Ministry of Education's written response to Parliamentary Question No 44, while part (b) concerning the decision-making process and the 60-year declassification timeline was covered in the Ministry of Digital Development and Information's oral reply to Parliamentary Question No 68 on 12 January 2026. No new amendments or explanations were introduced during this session.

results.title: Probe into Charities that Fail to Comply with Requirements under Code of Governance for Charities and Institutions of Public Character
results.summary: Over the past three years, the Office of the Commissioner of Charities (COC) received an average of 80 annual feedback items regarding governance and fundraising conduct across approximately 2,400 registered charities. The Code of Governance operates on a "comply or explain" basis, where non-compliance must be justified in annual submissions published on the Charity Portal. While most breaches stem from unfamiliarity with requirements, the COC issues advisories to assist charities; however, firmer action including Orders is taken for gross misconduct or mismanagement. In 2025, two Orders prohibited specific individuals from fundraising due to unfitness or lack of accountability. The COC continues to balance sector development with high governance standards to preserve public trust.

results.title: Probe into Similarities between Roles of Recently Retrenched Staff and Graduate Trainees of Digital Bank
results.summary: Workforce Singapore confirmed that roles retrenched at digital bank GXS were technical positions supporting completed IT projects, which differ from the intended Graduate Industry Traineeships (GRIT) roles. Although this distinction does not automatically disqualify GXS from the programme, the bank has voluntarily withdrawn from the GRIT initiative. Workforce Singapore remains committed to collaborating with leading companies to provide meaningful training opportunities for trainees.

results.title: Processing Times for Work Injury Compensation Claims Made by Platform Workers and Top Three Reasons for Rejections of Claims
results.summary: Platform workers' work injury compensation claims are processed within six months of the incident report submission, with designated insurers required to pay out within 21 days if no objections exist. The primary reason for claim rejections in 2025 was that the injury or disease did not arise out of and in the course of platform work, such as injuries sustained during personal errands. The Ministry tracks rejection reasons, identifying this eligibility constraint as the leading cause for denial.

results.title: Proposal for New Ministry to Coordinate Singapore's Energy Needs, Decarbonisation Pathways, Grid Resilience and International Negotiations
results.summary: The Government confirmed it has no plan to establish a separate Ministry of Energy. Instead, energy coordination is managed within the Ministry of Trade and Industry (MTI), which oversees the Energy Market Authority. To accelerate the transition, the Prime Minister designated Minister Dr Tan See Leng as Minister-in-charge of Energy last year. In April 2024, MTI created a new Energy and Carbon cluster to coordinate whole-of-Government efforts and build capabilities for decarbonisation, grid resilience, and international negotiations. The Government will continue refining this organisational structure as energy scale and complexity grow.

results.title: Proposal for Outpatient Primary Care Insurance Scheme for Migrant Domestic Workers
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower confirmed there are no plans to extend the Primary Care Plan (PCP) to migrant domestic workers, despite the scheme's 2022 launch for other sectors. Employers seeking cost predictability for outpatient care for these workers are advised to purchase voluntary comprehensive insurance plans with outpatient coverage instead. The Ministry maintains that the current PCP structure, which includes medical centres near dormitories, is not being expanded to this specific group at this time.

results.title: Protection for Employees and Whistle-blowers during Workplace Bullying Investigations
results.summary: The Tripartite Advisory encourages employers to prevent harassment and bullying while ensuring whistle-blowers face no penalties; workers facing retaliation may seek assistance from the Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices. Upon the Workplace Fairness Act's implementation at the end of 2027, employers will be mandated to establish grievance handling processes for harassment and discrimination, protect reporter confidentiality, and are prohibited from retaliating against those with genuine grievances. Non-compliance will attract financial penalties.

results.title: Protocols when Suspected Money Mule Activities are Flagged, and Incidence in Recent Years of Unauthorised Access to Phone Lines, Bank Accounts and Singpass
results.summary: The Anti-Scam Centre (ASC) coordinates with seven major banks and telecommunications companies to freeze accounts and terminate phone lines within hours to a day upon scam reports. Proactive fraud analytics are deployed by banks and telcos to detect and restrict suspected money mule activities before funds are siphoned. Since October 2025, approximately 1,200 individuals and 50 corporate entities have faced facility restrictions; those convicted or issued composition sums face one-year bans, while repeat offenders face three-year bans. Restrictions remain in place until case outcomes are determined for persons under investigation. Early results indicate effectiveness in preventing reoffending, though long-term assessment is pending.

results.title: Provisions Made in Public and Private Hospitals to Detect and Deal With Transmission of Infectious Airborne Diseases
results.summary: Public and private hospitals in Singapore maintain infection prevention and control systems aligned with National Infection Prevention and Control Guidelines to detect and manage infectious airborne diseases. Patients are triaged for respiratory symptoms at admission, monitored routinely, and isolated in single or cohort rooms if suspected or confirmed to have airborne infections. Public hospitals hold contingency plans for disease surges, with transitional care facilities adaptable as isolation units to augment capacity when required. Notifiable infectious diseases and hospital-acquired infection clusters are reported to the Communicable Diseases Agency (CDA), which collaborates with hospitals to ensure timely response measures. The public is advised to avoid healthcare visits when unwell, practice hand hygiene, and wear masks during visits to protect patients.

results.title: Health Information Bill
results.summary: The Health Information Bill establishes a statutory framework requiring all licensed healthcare providers and retail pharmacies to contribute key data to the National Electronic Health Record (NEHR) system to enable "One Patient, One Health Summary." The legislation permits scoped sharing of non-NEHR information without individual consent only for specified entities and use cases like continuity of care or public health outreach, while strictly prohibiting access by insurers and employers except for prescribed statutory medical examinations. To address concerns regarding cybersecurity burdens on small clinics and data privacy, the Ministry has issued draft guidelines on ethical principles and professional standards, positioning NEHR as a supplementary clinical tool rather than a mandatory step for every consultation. The Bill also incorporates "break-glass" access for emergencies and access restrictions managed solely by healthcare providers to protect sensitive data regarding mental health, STIs, and substance use.

results.title: Public Housing Options in Future Development of Pulau Brani
results.summary: Under the Sentosa Development Corporation's Greater Sentosa Master Plan, Pulau Brani is designated for development as a leisure and tourism destination. The Minister for National Development confirmed there are currently no plans to include public housing options in the future development of the island. This decision aligns with the specific zoning intended for tourism activities rather than residential public housing. No alternative timeline or eligibility criteria for public housing on Pulau Brani were stated.

results.title: Public Trust and Content Standards for State-Supported Digital Platforms
results.summary: The Government does not intervene in editorial positioning but expects all media, including STOMP, to uphold responsible journalism standards ensuring accuracy, fairness, and legal compliance. STOMP operates under the Online News Licensing Scheme, requiring licensees to adhere to content standards safeguarding racial and religious harmony, public order, and good taste. Members with specific concerns regarding STOMP's content are directed to write in directly to the outlet rather than seeking government intervention.

results.title: Publication of Quarterly Statistics on AI-related Retrenchment and Redeployment of Affected Workers
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) does not currently publish quarterly statistics specifically isolating AI-driven retrenchments, as business restructuring—which encompasses AI adoption—accounts for 60% to 70% of retrenchments since 2024. MOM surveys capture restructuring reasons broadly rather than distinguishing AI as a standalone category. The Government will continue studying the impact of technological adoption on employment trends but has not committed to releasing the requested granular data on redeployment rates or re-entry timelines for AI-affected workers.

results.title: Reasons for Early Tenancy Termination of Timbre at One Punggol Hawker Centre
results.summary: On 14 August 2026, Timbre and the National Environment Agency (NEA) mutually agreed to terminate Timbre's tenancy at One Punggol Hawker Centre due to operational challenges. This timeline allows NEA to appoint a new operator and ensure an orderly transition with minimal disruption to stallholders and patrons. There are no plans for NEA to directly manage the centre, which will continue to operate as a Socially-conscious Enterprise Hawker Centre. The Minister confirmed these details in response to questions regarding the reasons for early termination and future management arrangements.

results.title: Reasons for Lack of Lift Installation Plans for Pedestrian Overhead Bridge near Block 301D Anchorvale Road
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority considers the density of seniors in the vicinity when prioritising lift retrofits for pedestrian overhead bridges. Although the bridge near Block 301D is located on Anchorvale Drive rather than Anchorvale Road, it meets the senior-density criterion. Consequently, the bridge will be retrofitted with a lift within the next few years. The Minister confirmed that this specific location was included in the retrofitting schedule based on the applicable eligibility conditions.

results.title: Reasons for Masjid Al-Firdaus Closure
results.summary: Masjid Al-Firdaus in Choa Chu Kang will continue operating on its Temporary Occupation Licence until the new Tengah mosque is completed in the early 2030s, ensuring uninterrupted prayer access for servicemen and foreign workers. The new facility will offer larger capacity, enhanced facilities, and a central location with improved transport links to serve the Bukit Batok and Choa Chu Kang communities. MUIS plans to preserve the existing mosque's heritage by documenting its history and continuing its traditions within the new design, rather than retaining the physical structure on land designated for future development. Further redevelopment plans for the site will be shared when finalized.

results.title: Reasons for Omission of Hearing Loss Data in National Population Health Survey and Auditory Tests under Healthier SG for Early Intervention
results.summary: The 2024 National Population Health Survey omits hearing loss data as it focuses on chronic diseases and lifestyle risk factors, with the latest self-reported prevalence figures available from 2023. Objective audiometry screening is not recommended for the general population per the Screening Test Review Committee 2019 guidelines but remains advised for high-risk groups like those exposed to excessive noise. To support early detection among seniors, Project Silver Screen screens individuals aged 60 and above for vision, hearing, and oral health since 2018. Identified abnormalities trigger referrals to community hearing clinics for further checks and provision of assistive devices such as hearing aids.
