Healthcare professionals manage suspected cases through investigation and specialist referrals as needed.

results.title: Recouping Losses Suffered by State Investment Entities
results.summary: The Minister clarified that GIC and Temasek, as commercial entities, must reserve legal rights to safeguard investment value and pursue litigation where appropriate. Decisions to sue depend on relevant facts, legal merits, applicable laws, and a cost-benefit analysis of dispute resolution options. The Government expects these entities to take legal action to recoup losses when justified by commercial considerations and fiduciary duties. No specific criteria, loss quantum thresholds, or time periods for commencing lawsuits were disclosed in the response.

results.title: Reducing Breast Cancer Screening Age Limit for Early Detection and Subsidies for Women under 50
results.summary: Under Healthier SG, fully subsidised breast cancer screening is currently available to women aged 50 to 69 every two years. Women aged 40 to 49 may also access screening if their doctor deems it clinically appropriate. The Ministry of Health noted that breast cancer incidence has risen across age groups, including women in their 40s, and will continue to review evidence and screening recommendations. No specific timeline for lowering the age limit to 45 was stated, and subsidies for women under 50 remain contingent on clinical assessment rather than a fixed policy change.

results.title: Review of Legal Profession Act 1966 Given Law Society's Recent Extraordinary General Meeting Resolutions
results.summary: The Ministry for Law will consider amendments to the Legal Profession Act 1966 following resolutions passed at the Law Society's extraordinary general meeting on 22 December 2025. This review is part of the Ministry's regular process to ensure the Act's continued relevance. The Ministry has noted the EGM points and will evaluate them alongside inputs from other fora. No specific amendments or timelines were confirmed during this session.

results.title: Review of Major Games Award Programme
results.summary: The Acting Minister clarified that the Major Games Award Programme (MAP) is a private scheme managed by the Singapore National Olympic Council (SNOC) with sponsor funding, meaning the Government does not directly control its review timeline or specific monetary rewards. While the Government does not update MAP cash incentives directly, it provides holistic athlete support through Sport Excellence initiatives like spexCarding and spexScholarship, which offer grants and monthly stipends independent of medal outcomes. The Minister noted that SNOC periodically reviews the MAP structure based on competition levels and frequency, such as the four-year Olympic cycle versus the two-year SEA Games, and encourages corporate sponsors to contribute. Recognition for non-medal achievements is also addressed through the Singapore Sport Hall of Fame, which now includes a category for sport leaders.

results.title: Review of PayNow Bank Account Linking Procedures for Recycled Mobile Numbers
results.summary: The Monetary Authority of Singapore maintains that current procedures for linking and delinking recycled mobile numbers to PayNow bank accounts are sufficient. No enhancements to the process are deemed necessary as of the assessment provided on 16 October 2024. The Government's position aligns with the existing operational framework, indicating no immediate policy changes or new constraints regarding recycled numbers for PayNow transactions.

results.title: Review of Political Office Holders' Salaries Following 2023 Deferral
results.summary: The Government has convened an independent committee chaired by Mr Gan Seow Kee to review political office holders' salaries, as the 2023 scheduled review was deferred due to global economic uncertainties. The 2012 salary framework, which mandates annual adjustments based on benchmark salaries, remains unchanged since its introduction. The committee will recommend appropriate salary levels and propose refinements to ensure the framework's relevance. Upon receiving the committee's report, the Government will consider its findings and provide an update to Parliament. The review process is currently underway, with no specific timeline for the report's submission or subsequent salary adjustments stated at this time.

results.title: Review of Three-Year Suspension of Primary 1 Student Intake Imposed on Madrasahs Given Consistent Successful PSLE Performance
results.summary: Under the Compulsory Education Act, the four designated madrasahs must meet PSLE benchmarks at least twice within every three-year assessment period to maintain Primary 1 student intake eligibility. The Minister confirmed that failure to meet this requirement results in a three-year prohibition on intake. This rule has applied since 2011, and the four madrasahs have consistently satisfied the benchmark since 2021. The requirement remains in force to ensure students in madrasahs maintain a firm academic foundation.

results.title: Robustness of Trials Where Nets Were Placed in 35 Flats in Punggol and Toa Payoh to Prevent Pigeon Nesting Behaviour
results.summary: HDB is conducting a trial on 35 units in Punggol and Toa Payoh to evaluate half-height netting solutions for preventing pigeon nesting at air-conditioner ledges. The selection criteria considered Town Council and resident feedback regarding persistent nesting issues. Findings from this trial will be shared with all Town Councils to determine if the solution can be scaled up islandwide to complement NParks' management efforts. The study aims to assess the robustness of the netting before wider implementation.

results.title: Safeguards for Employees whose Organisations Enforce Work-in-office Arrangements
results.summary: Under the Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangement Requests (TG-FWAR) implemented in December 2024, employees may formally request flexible work arrangements, including flexi-place, flexi-time, and flexi-load, regardless of whether their default arrangement is on-site. Employers must consider these requests based on business needs. Employees whose requests are not properly assessed may approach the Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices (TAFEP) for assistance. If employers fail to comply with the guidelines after TAFEP's assessment, they will be guided to rectify the situation.

results.title: Safety Lapses in Two Recent Fall-from-height Deaths for Construction of Jurong Region Line
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) is investigating two fatal fall-from-height incidents during the Jurong Region Line construction and cannot yet disclose specific safety lapses. Work-at-height activities are governed by the Workplace Safety and Health (Work at Heights) Regulations, mandating physical controls like guardrails, fall prevention plans, permit-to-work systems, and adequate supervision. Contractors must comply with these requirements and refer to the Approved Code of Practice for guidance. MOM will continue to learn from these incidents and implement additional safety measures where required to prevent similar occurrences across the project.

results.title: Statistics for Incidents and Accidents Involving PMDs and PABs
results.summary: From 2023 to Q3 2025, approximately 710 reported accidents involving personal mobility aids, devices, or power-assisted bicycles resulted in injuries. Of these incidents, roughly 590 occurred on roads, while about 120 happened off-road on public paths and shared pathways. The Acting Minister for Transport provided these figures in response to a query regarding grievous injuries sustained during this period. No further breakdown of injury severity or specific incident types was disclosed in the statement.

results.title: Stepping Up Public Education Efforts in Light of Residential Unit Fires in 2025 and Review of Singapore’s Building, Fire and Construction Safety Codes
results.summary: In 2025, Singapore recorded 1,051 residential fires, a slight increase from 968 in 2024, though the fire incidence rate relative to total dwelling stock decreased to 0.065%. The Government is reviewing early observations from Hong Kong's high-rise fire investigations to potentially strengthen regulations for construction and maintenance works. To enhance public preparedness, the Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) conducts approximately 16 Community Resilience Days annually and the Responder Plus Programme, which saw over 60,000 participants between January 2024 and August 2025. SCDF responds to most incidents within eight minutes, utilizing fire lifts in buildings above 24 metres. For seniors, the Enhancement for Active Seniors Programme subsidises Home Fire Alarm Devices to provide earlier evacuation warnings.

results.title: Strengthening Enforcement Actions Taken against Illegal Exhaust Modifications or Breaches of Noise Emission Standards
results.summary: In the past two years, approximately 1,600 and 350 vehicle owners were penalised for illegal exhaust modifications and noise emission breaches respectively. The Land Transport and Related Matters Bill, scheduled for introduction in the January Parliamentary Sitting, will implement heavier penalties for illegal exhaust system modifications. These measures address concerns regarding the disproportionate impact of vehicle noise on residents' sleep and mental wellbeing. No specific timeline for enforcement strengthening beyond the new penalties was stated.

results.title: Strengthening Protection for Consumers who Purchase High Value Packages from Businesses with Knowledge of Their Insolvency
results.summary: Under the Consumer Protection (Fair Trading) Act 2003, it is an unfair practice for suppliers to exert undue pressure or accept prepayments when they know they cannot supply goods or services. The Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore can enforce against such suppliers, while consumers may seek redress via the Consumers Association of Singapore (CASE). CASE offers a scheme accrediting businesses committed to fair trading and claims on unused prepayments upon closure. In March 2025, the Government convened an independent Consumer Protection Review Panel to review protections for high-value package purchases, with findings and recommendations expected later this year. The Government will study these recommendations to consider necessary additional measures.

results.title: Support to Create National Service-friendly Workplace Environments
results.summary: The Ministry of Defence (MINDEF) and Singapore Armed Forces (SAF) utilise the Advisory Council on Community Relations in Defence (ACCORD) to refine policies supporting employers, with specific focus on small and medium enterprises (SMEs). Advance notice for staff coverage is provided six months prior to high-key In-Camp Training (ICT) and three months for low-key ICT, arrangements developed based on employer feedback. ACCORD facilitates the Supportive Employers Networking Session for best practice exchange and manages recognition schemes including the NS Mark for HR practices and the Total Defence Award for exemplary support, with special efforts to identify SME awardees. MINDEF and SAF will continue collaborating with ACCORD to enhance support for employers and National Servicemen.

results.title: Public Sector (Governance) (Amendment) Bill
results.summary: The Public Sector (Governance) (Amendment) Bill introduces a controlled framework for public agencies to share data with trusted external partners, such as social service agencies, solely for legitimate public purposes. This mechanism requires specific Ministerial authorisation and mandates that partners adhere to strict contractual Terms of Use covering data protection and security, explicitly prohibiting commercial exploitation or overriding existing consent laws. While the Government supports a tiered, risk-based approach to balance efficiency with safety, it clarified that organisational statutory liability for non-personal data misuse does not currently exist, relying instead on contractual enforcement. Requests for a public register of Ministerial directions and a five-year review were noted, though the Government indicated these would be considered only after gaining sufficient operational experience with the new provisions.

results.title: Supporting SMEs to Adapt to Global Emphasis on Sustainability and Green Technologies
results.summary: The Ministry supports SMEs adapting to global sustainability demands through the SME Sustainability Hub, a digital platform launched in May offering one-stop access to resources. Key measures include the SME Sustainability Reporting Programme and Sustainability Reporting Grant to assist with financial and corporate environmental standards, alongside the Enterprise Sustainability Programme providing training on decarbonisation and sustainable finance. Funding via the Enterprise Development Grant further aids SMEs in developing sustainability strategies, green products, and adopting technologies. The Government collaborates with trade associations to continually review these schemes, ensuring Singapore businesses can thrive in a low-carbon future while maintaining international competitiveness.

results.title: Sustainable Indicators for Changes under New Integrated Shield Plan Rider Design Requirements
results.summary: The Minister for Health directed that questions regarding specific sustainability indicators for Integrated Shield Plan riders and projected out-of-pocket spending figures be addressed in the oral reply to Questions 1–4 at the 12 January 2026 Parliament Sitting. The transcript does not contain the specific indicators, modelled spending changes, or projected figures requested, as these details were provided in a separate oral response not included in this text. The new rider design requirements are confirmed to take effect on 1 April 2026.

results.title: Take-up Rates of Human Papillomavirus Vaccination by Gender and Age Groups for Past Five Years
results.summary: The HPV vaccination is nationally recommended only for females aged 9 to 26 years to prevent cervical cancer, with a school-based programme launched in April 2019. Vaccination rates for females are tracked via the Department of Statistics Singapore and Communicable Diseases Surveillance reports, whereas male coverage is not tracked as there is no current national recommendation for males. Although the vaccine offers protection against HPV-related cancers in males, incidence rates are significantly lower than cervical cancer in females, justifying the current policy focus on females. High-risk males may consult doctors for individual vaccination options.

results.title: Tapping SkillsFuture Credits for Alternative Uses for Persons with Special Needs
results.summary: From 2022 to 2024, approximately 500 persons with disabilities annually accessed training support via SG Enable and SkillsFuture Singapore, including course fee subsidies up to 95% and SkillsFuture credits for out-of-pocket fees. SG Enable's Capability Development Grant assists providers in adapting programmes using assistive devices and software to meet varied disability needs. Both agencies will collaborate with more training providers and actively reach out to additional persons with disabilities to expand participation. Training providers may submit accessible courses for SkillsFuture credit eligibility, increasing available options. The Minister confirmed these measures but did not specify alternative credit conversions for those unable to access approved programmes.

results.title: Temasek Holdings' Planned Restructuring and Potential Impact on Investment Performance and Returns
results.summary: The Government does not direct Temasek Holdings' individual investment decisions or benchmark it to a specific reference portfolio, expecting instead sustainable long-term returns commensurate with its active, bottom-up risk profile. While unlisted assets are valued conservatively at book value less impairment, Temasek's 20-year annualised total shareholder return of approximately 7% aligns with global benchmarks like the ACWI IMI and peer sovereign wealth funds such as the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board. The Minister clarified that Temasek is not expected to accept lower financial returns for strategic outcomes, noting that inherent volatility is acceptable provided returns remain sustainable and aligned with its mandate. The Government will not interfere in how Temasek manages its mix of listed and unlisted assets or directs dividend usage, leaving such operational decisions to Temasek management.

results.title: Ten-Year Trend Analysis of Mental Health Condition Diagnosis Detection among National Servicemen
results.summary: Minister of State for Defence Mr Desmond Choo noted a general upward trend in full-time National Servicemen seeking mental health support, consistent with national youth mental health patterns. While specific percentages for diagnoses before enlistment versus during service and a decade-ago comparison were requested, the provided response highlighted this overall increase rather than detailing the exact figures. The latest data, referenced in a Parliamentary Question from 4 November 2025, indicates that annual proportions vary but reflect a broader national trend.

results.title: Time Limit for Senior Minister of State's Speech
results.summary: Members moved to exempt proceedings from the time limit under Standing Order No 48(8) for Senior Minister of State Tan Kiat How's speech. The request seeks general assent to remove the standard time constraint specifically for his contribution. No further details regarding the speech content or the outcome of the motion were provided in the transcript.

results.title: Total Disbursements through Sports Excellence Schemes
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth stated that direct financial and programme support under the Sport Excellence (spex) suite is bespoke and holistic, tailored to athletes' needs across different career stages. Currently, the Government supports over 1,500 carded athletes across 55 sports, representing a 450% increase in athlete count and a 300% increase in sports coverage since the spex programme began in 1993. Recent enhancements include the 2024 introduction of spexPotential for younger athletes, inaugural spexEducation Undergraduate Scholarships last year, and CPF top-ups for spexScholars. Support levels are calibrated annually based on individual needs, performance, and potential, with the Government reviewing policies to ensure adequacy while encouraging corporate and public support through the One Team Singapore Fund.

results.title: Training Nurses to Provide Care Beyond Inpatient Settings Given Push for Community Care
results.summary: The Ministry of Health is incorporating cross-disciplinary competencies into nursing and physician training to support community care. Nursing curricula now include nutrition intervention modules for chronic conditions, while community nurses are trained to detect functional problems like impaired mobility for prompt escalation. Residency training for family and rehabilitation physicians includes identifying community rehabilitation services to refer patients, facilitating inter-professional collaboration for holistic care. These measures align with the push to expand nursing roles beyond inpatient settings and enhance upstream collaboration among healthcare providers.

results.title: Training for Teachers to Deal With Disclosures of Mental Health Challenges by Students Who Also Refuse Parental or Counsellor Involvement
results.summary: Teachers receive basic counselling training covering confidentiality limits and escalation protocols when students disclose mental health challenges. While teachers must explain the rationale for additional support, they are obligated to inform school leaders regarding safety concerns like self-harm or abuse, triggering referrals to counsellors, community services, or parents. The Ministry of Health has convened an expert group to study parental consent issues and will release youth sector guidelines soon. The Ministry of Education will review these guidelines for applicability to schools, though specific timelines for their release remain pending.

results.title: Unutilised SkillsFuture Credits and Transfer Options to Immediate Family Members
results.summary: The Minister stated that unused SkillsFuture Credits are not transferable between individuals, including immediate family members, to encourage individual ownership of lifelong learning. Singaporeans aged 25 and above receive a $500 opening credit, while those aged 40 and above received a $4,000 mid-career top-up starting May 2024. As of December 2024, more than one in three eligible Singaporeans have utilised their credits, with average utilised amounts of approximately $330 for the base tier and $420 for the mid-career tier. Data for December 2025 will be available in February 2026. Individuals who exhaust their credits remain eligible for up to 90% course fee subsidies for SSG-supported programmes.

results.title: Update Code of Ethics and Professional Client Care for Property Agents to Declare Use of AI-edited Images and Videos of Property Listings
results.summary: The Council for Estate Agencies (CEA) requires property agents to provide disclaimers for AI-edited images or videos in property advertisements to prevent misleading consumers. The CEA is currently reviewing its regulatory guidelines to clarify the use of artificial intelligence in property listings, aiming to balance consumer protection with industry innovation. This review will update the Code of Ethics and Professional Client Care to specifically address the declaration of AI usage in property marketing materials.

results.title: Update on Investigation into Accident Involving Two Public Buses along Jurong West Ave 1
results.summary: All persons injured and hospitalized in the 14 December 2025 accident involving two public buses on Jurong West Avenue 1 have been discharged. The two involved bus operators are currently assisting injured passengers with their medical claims. Investigations into the incident are ongoing, and the Land Transport Authority will provide further updates when developments occur. No specific financial support amounts or investigation completion timelines were stated at this time.

results.title: Upstream Preventive Support Enhancements for Vulnerable Households under ComLink+ Towards Better Social Mobility
results.summary: The Minister stated that indicators for family stability and social mobility are published in the Supporting Lower-Income Households Trends Report 2025. Under ComLink+, Family Service Centre case workers and family coaches identify high-need households to deliver upstream preventive support, including preschool enrolment and KidSTART. These measures target children and caregivers in vulnerable families to address persistent challenges in family functioning and child well-being. Insights from recent outcomes inform ongoing enhancements to these support programmes.
